name: Borriol Castle and Racó de Raca Loop
name_original: El Racó de Raca — Circular desde Borriol
description: Hike the moderate 8.8-mile El Raco de Raca circular trail from Borriol, gaining 1340 feet with diverse scenery and historical sites.
region_intro: Hiking around Borriol offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by mountains, valleys, and forests. The region is situated between the Desert de les Palmes Natural Park and the Serra de Borriol, with the Borriol river flowing through its base. This topography provides a variety of trails, from wooded areas to elevated viewpoints, showcasing traditional dry-stone buildings and historical sites. Best hiking trails around Borriol The most popular hiking route is El Raco de Raca — Circular desde Borriol, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Tossal Gros Viewpoint – View from Tossal Gros loop from Borriol, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. It leads to panoramic viewpoints, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Cruz de la Roca de la Dama – Roca de la Dama loop from Borriol, a 3.3 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Borriol is defined by its local mountain ranges, lush forests, and historical landmarks like Borriol Castle. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Borriol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Borriol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Borriol?
A: Borriol offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 35 trails to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Borriol?
A: Hikes around Borriol feature diverse natural landscapes, including mountains, valleys, and forests. You'll find trails winding through wooded areas, leading to elevated viewpoints, and passing traditional dry-stone buildings. The region is situated between the &lt;strong&gt;Desert de les Palmes Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Borriol&lt;/strong&gt;, offering a rich blend of natural beauty.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners in Borriol?
A: While most routes in Borriol are moderately challenging, there is at least one easy trail available, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. The diverse network of trails ensures options for various ability levels.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Borriol?
A: Yes, many of Borriol's hiking trails are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5308546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Roca de la Dama – Roca de la Dama loop from Borriol&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Borriol?
A: Borriol is rich in history. You can encounter the remains of &lt;strong&gt;Borriol Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, perched on a large rock, offering spectacular panoramic views. The area also preserves significant Roman remains, as the ancient Via Augusta Roman road passed through Borriol, and prehistoric and Iberian civilization sites add to the historical depth. The &lt;strong&gt;La Joquera Route&lt;/strong&gt;, for instance, guides hikers through ancient cave paintings and Iberian settlements.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic views on Borriol's trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its impressive vistas. The &lt;strong&gt;Remolins Viewing Point&lt;/strong&gt; offers sweeping panoramic views of Borriol and its surroundings, including the prominent Borriol Castle. Another popular route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tossal Gros Viewpoint – View from Tossal Gros loop from Borriol&lt;/a&gt;, leads to expansive panoramic viewpoints.

Q: What natural features are prominent in Borriol's hiking areas?
A: Borriol's hiking areas are defined by the &lt;strong&gt;Desert de les Palmes Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by mountains and unique flora, including the European fan palm. The local mountain range, &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Borriol&lt;/strong&gt;, provides a picturesque backdrop with beautiful wooded areas and charming spots like the Pou del Portugués, an embosomed path with a fountain. The Borriol river also flows through the base of the municipality.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Borriol?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the variety of trails suitable for different skill levels. The panoramic views from elevated points are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Borriol?
A: Yes, many popular trailheads in and around Borriol offer convenient parking options, making it easier to access the hiking routes. Specific parking details can often be found on individual route descriptions.

Q: How long do hikes around Borriol typically take?
A: Hikes around Borriol vary in length and duration. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877553880/el-raco-de-raca-circular-desde-borriol" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Raco de Raca — Circular desde Borriol&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 14.2 km, typically takes around 4 hours and 26 minutes. Shorter trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14713877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Skull Rock – Endobeles Sculpture loop from Borriol&lt;/a&gt; (8.7 km), can be completed in about 3 hours.

Q: What is the Desert de les Palmes Natural Park, and how does it relate to hiking in Borriol?
A: The &lt;strong&gt;Desert de les Palmes Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; is a protected natural space just a few kilometers from Borriol. It's characterized by mountains and offers impressive views, including glimpses of the sea. The park is an integral part of Borriol's natural surroundings, providing numerous hiking opportunities amidst its unique flora, notably the European fan palm. You can find more information about the park on &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Desert_de_les_Palmes_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are Borriol's hiking trails suitable for families?
A: Yes, Borriol offers trails suitable for families, including easier walks that allow for a pleasant outdoor experience with children. The diverse landscape and historical points of interest along some routes can also make for an engaging family adventure.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Borriol?
A: Many of Borriol's natural trails are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially within protected natural areas, and to keep your dog on a leash.
tips: From its 458 meters above sea level, the Racó de Raca offers wonderful views in all directions, especially towards the coast of Castellón. You get there by several alternative paths each with more or less difficulty but they are usually easy to get around. It is a place also popular with runners.

It is a fortress with Roman origins and structures of Islamic and medieval architecture, located on a hill north of the city.
It is likely that it was a watchtower in Roman times, which optically connected to other nearby towers and whose task was the safety of Augusta Street.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead for El Raco de Raca — Circular desde Borriol?
faqs.answer: The starting point for this circular route is conveniently located next to a parking area in Borriol. This makes it easily accessible if you're traveling by car.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to hike the El Raco de Raca — Circular desde Borriol trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, the varied terrain and potential for warm weather suggest that spring and autumn might offer the most comfortable hiking conditions. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and pleasant scenery.

faqs.question: Is the El Raco de Raca — Circular desde Borriol trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your canine companion along. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near other hikers, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: This 14.2-kilometer circular route features diverse natural landscapes, including mountains, valleys, and forests. You'll encounter varied terrain, which means a mix of surfaces, but the paths are mostly accessible. Be prepared for some significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich variety of natural scenery and cultural elements. You'll pass by the impressive Racó de Raca Summit, providing elevated viewpoints. You can also explore the historical Castillo de Borriol and may encounter traditional dry-stone constructions, adding a cultural dimension to your hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The El Raco de Raca — Circular desde Borriol trail is classified as moderate difficulty. It requires a good physical condition and a steady step due to its varied terrain and significant elevation gain. While not ideal for absolute beginners, experienced families or those with older, active children might find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as Ermita la Magdalena - Ermita Sant Vicent - Ermita la Magdalena, Almenara - les Coves de Vinromà, and the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana, among others.

faqs.question: What makes this particular trail notable in the Borriol area?
faqs.answer: This trail is highlighted as the most popular hiking route in Borriol. Its popularity stems from its diverse landscapes, offering a mix of forested areas and elevated viewpoints, as well as the cultural and historical elements like dry-stone constructions and the Borriol Castle.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a map or GPS device. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are also advisable.
